findings.desc: Arbitrary `from` parameter lets an attacker steal tokens from any address that approved the bridge, locking them to an L2 recipient they control.
Signed message allows arbitrary `target` and `data` calls, so a malicious/compromised signer can invoke `transferFrom` to drain the entire vault to any address.
findings.counterexample: assertion fails with no symbolic input (trivial witness)

findings.desc: An attacker can deposit vault-held tokens back to themselves (using vault as `from` via the bridge's own allowance) to infinitely mint L2 tokens with no L1 lock.
No replay protection (nonce/used-signature tracking), allowing the same signed withdrawal to be replayed repeatedly to drain funds.

proposer.raw:
CRITICAL | L1BossBridge.depositTokensToL2 | Arbitrary `from` parameter lets an attacker steal tokens from any address that approved the bridge, locking them to an L2 recipient they control.
CRITICAL | L1BossBridge.sendToL1 | Signed message allows arbitrary `target` and `data` calls, so a malicious/compromised signer can invoke `transferFrom` to drain the entire vault to any address.
HIGH | L1BossBridge.depositTokensToL2 | An attacker can deposit vault-held tokens back to themselves (using vault as `from` via the bridge's own allowance) to infinitely mint L2 tokens with no L1 lock.
HIGH | L1BossBridge.withdrawTokensToL1 | No replay protection (nonce/used-signature tracking), allowing the same signed withdrawal to be replayed repeatedly to drain funds.

results.finding: a signed withdrawal can be replayed to drain the vault.
results.counterexample: assertion fails with no symbolic input (trivial witness)
